release_review.summary: Workbench default widget sizing now gives Files, Terminal, and Monitoring more usable first-run space while preserving the existing Runtime Service protocol window.
checked_surfaces: Runtime Service snapshot schema, protocol version, compatibility epoch, minimum Desktop version, and minimum Runtime version are unchanged from v0.6.13
Desktop attach/start flows still normalize startup reports through the existing Runtime Service snapshot path without changing required Runtime Service fields
Local UI health and sys.ping continue to return the same required compatibility fields, with no new Runtime Service JSON semantics required by the Workbench sizing change
Env App Workbench initial canvas, widget catalog defaults, widget persistence, and add-widget sizing were reviewed as UI layout behavior rather than Runtime Service protocol behavior
Env App maintenance UI, settings surfaces, file browser, terminal, monitoring, wheel ownership, and text selection ownership keep their existing Runtime Service expectations
SSH runtime bootstrap, remote startup report handling, runtime-control endpoint discovery, provider link, and open readiness continue to consume the same normalized Runtime Service snapshot fields
release_review.same_window_rationale: The Runtime Service contract itself has no source changes in this release: protocol_version remains redeven-runtime-v1, compatibility_epoch remains 5, and both minimum version fields remain v0.7.0. The release changes adjust Workbench widget catalog default sizes and first-run canvas placement so Files, Terminal, and Monitoring have more usable visual space; those values are local Env App layout metadata and do not add, remove, or reinterpret Runtime Service snapshot fields. Desktop attach/start, Local UI health, startup reports, sys.ping, Env App maintenance UI, SSH runtime bootstrap, provider link, runtime-control, and open readiness were reviewed with no compatibility-window change required.
